Notice Of Motion And Motion For Waiver Of Undertaking
Off Calendar Nov-07-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jeffrey B. Demain, Esq., of Altshuler Berzon, LLP, 415-421-7151, for defendants and opposing party UHW-W and David Regan

No other Appearance. Tentative Ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFFS SAL ROSSELLI, JOHN BORSOS, RALPH CORNEJO, ANGELA LASPER, BEVERLY GRIFFITH, JAN HARRIS AND IRMA DUFELMEIER'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR WAIVER OF UNDERTAKING is Granted in part and denied in part. Relief from the undertaking requirement is denied. (See Dowling v. Zimmerman (2001) 85 Cal.App.4th 1400, 1432). Enforcement of the fee order is stayed until December 12, 2011 to allow plaintiff time to obtain a bond. Defendant to receive $2500 as reasonable attorney's fees for opposing this simple motion. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: Lavena Ward, CSR 7077

MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Oct-03-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEVEN L. ROBINSON OF THE LAW OFFICES OF ALIOTO, TEL. NO. 434-8700 EXT. 809 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JONATHAN WEISSGLASS OF THE ALTSHULER BERZON LLP, TEL. NO. 421-7151 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANTS SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, UHW-W, ANDY STERN, ANNA BURGER, MARY HENRY, DAVID REGAN AND ELISEO MEDINA'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND LITIGATION EXPENSES is granted in part. Defendants are awarded all of the expenses they seek, but only 35% of the attorneys' fees they seek. Fees and costs are recoverable under the anti-SLAPP statute even if the dismissed claims were brought under the Ralph and Bane Acts. Defendants are entitled to be made whole, which is what the fees award does. The dollar amount of the fees sought for both the anti-SLAPP motion and this motion are not reasonable given the nature of the work involved. TOTAL AMOUNT AWARDED IS $57,890.45. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7770 =302/HEK
